title: RF / SoC Debug Playbook
script:
Hardware engineers dread not the bug they cannot find, but the one they cannot locate. This post walks through the systematic debug taxonomy I use on Wi-Fi and RF SoC production lines so every new case gets triaged fast.

Let us start with the hardware layer. In any new product introduction, the most common failures are cross-layer interactions. The PCB design passes review, but SMT process variation breaks an entire batch. ATE passes every unit, yet the system hits a firmware corner case in the field.

If this resonated with you, subscribe and drop your worst hardware bug story in the comments below.
